translation: Those who (are) the head of the demons and the demon of Mazan and all druziin who unjustly and aggressively send their troops forward not for their own advantage and assistance but because they have an unworthy hatred and unusual envy; in order to accomplish (their) desire for QUE STION 36 129 annibilating the light entities by (inflicting) unworthy harm on them, they fight in a hard and violent struggle against the good creatures of the yazdiin.

translation: For if one said that (Christ) died, (the opponent and rival) said that he did not die; and when one said that he did not die, the opponent and rival said that he did. sourceParagraph: dd36.99a ud Druz pad ān ī xwēš zēn ud pad ān ī xwēš rānišn ān ī xwēš [D7_212] kōxšēnīdārīh ud ān ī dādār [TD4_268] xwarrah ud ān ī yazdān pērōzīhēnd čē nēst bawēd andar čār nēst bawānd ud hamist arwēsīhēnd aweštābīhēnd ud ānābīhēnd
translation: The druz, with her own weapons, her own +fighting, her own incitement to struggle, will be vanquished through the splendour of the Creator and the power of the yazdiin. All of them (i.e. druziin) will be twisted, oppressed and weakened. That is because, experiencing much pam and with full experience, they will be agitated, while coming back, through the separation of faculties. Just as with regard to the mortal material beings the soul is separated from the body by decay, by a separation of faculties, and by weakening of powers, that is because of its becoming stunned, ineffective, and motionless. It is similar to the shaking of an individual being of fine appearance, which is buried and is without power. At (reaching) equality (i.e. having reached equanimity?) (and through) their desire to shake, they are thrown outside. No part, or part of a part, of druzih nature, not even as much as a small part which is a third of a portion, which is within this enclosure of light, will remain of the nature of druzih.

sourceParagraph: dd36.99c ud ēd čē stardīhā ud a-kārīhā ud a-ǰumbāgīhā mānāg ō stī ī čihrīg wizandišn ī nigān abar nē ādūg ud abar hāwandīh kām čandišnīh [T60_60v] bērōn abganīhēnd ud ēč bahr ud bahrān bahr ī az druzīh ud tā-iz čand xwurdag ī a-baxšišn andar ēn rōšnīh parwast az druzīh nē mānēd
translation: The druz, with her own weapons, her own +fighting, her own incitement to struggle, will be vanquished through the splendour of the Creator and the power of the yazdiin. All of them (i.e. druziin) will be twisted, oppressed and weakened. That is because, experiencing much pam and with full experience, they will be agitated, while coming back, through the separation of faculties. Just as with regard to the mortal material beings the soul is separated from the body by decay, by a separation of faculties, and by weakening of powers, that is because of its becoming stunned, ineffective, and motionless. It is similar to the shaking of an individual being of fine appearance, which is buried and is without power. At (reaching) equality (i.e. having reached equanimity?) (and through) their desire to shake, they are thrown outside. No part, or part of a part, of druzih nature, not even as much as a small part which is a third of a portion, which is within this enclosure of light, will remain of the nature of druzih.

sourceParagraph: dd36.101a pas az Fraškerd nē bawēd dēw čē nē bawēd [K35_149v] frēb ud nē Druz čē nēst drōzišn [D7_214] nē bawēd angraman čē nēst zadārīh nē bawēd dōšox čē nēst druwandīh nē bawēd kōxšišn čē nēst Xešm ud nē bawēd kēn čē nēst wizāy ud nē bawēd dard čē nēst wēmārīh nē bawēd čēhāg čē nēst bīm nē bawēd niyāz čē nēst Āz nē bawēd šarm čē nēst zištīh ud nē bawēd drōw čē nēst drōw-kām nē bawēd ag-dēnīh čē nēst drōw-gōwišnīh
translation: After the renovation there is no druz since there is no deceit and there is no druz since there is no falsehood, there is no Ahreman since fuere is no destruction, there is no hell since there is no wickedness, there is no struggle since there is no anger, there is no malice since there is no harm, there is no pain since there is no disease, there is no grief since there is no fear, there is no need since there is no lust, there is no shame since there is no ugliness, there is no lie since there is no desire to lie, there is no infidelity since there is no false speech, and there is no evil since (its) offspring is destroyed in that it is said that the race of all evil thoughts and all evil speech and all evil deeds should be tom apatt by the Evil Spirit. (36.10 2) By his(i.e.Ahreman) destruction every evil is destroyed, by the destruction of evil every goodness is perfected, and during the time of perfect goodness it is not possible to allot any pain or harm by any means to any crearure.

sourceParagraph: dd36.103a ān ī nōg ka must ō tan pahikōbišn ayāb tēx burrāg bē ō handām zanīhēd āmēxtagīh ī ǰud-gōhr Druz andar tan ō abzārān rāy ud burrišn rēš zāyēd
translation: At the present time when a fist hits the bndy, a cutting blade strikes a limb, there is born (from this) a cut and a wound because of the fact that the druz of evil substance who is in the body is mixed within the faculties (of man). At that time of harmonious law (i.e. the time of eschatology), when one limb hits another, or something like a knife, sword, mace, stone, or arrow gets to the body. there is no pain or trouble, but against the new pain at that time bliss will come. For at the present time the pain that comes from smiting and hitting is always due to the one that is of evil substance and that is transient. At that time (i.e. after the renovation) all (beings) will be with the same substance and with the same constitution, and there will never be affliction. (36.1 04) In that most good time there will be sun, moon and shining (stars), but there will be no need for light, revolution (of the luminaries), and diffusion of luminosity, because the whole world will be light, bright, and there will be light to all the creatures. sourceParagraph: dd93.3 ud amāh hāmōyēn mardōmān abēr ōšyār bawišn kū tā az ān rāh bē nē ēstam bē ō rāh ī dēwān druzān nē šawam
meaning: General term for demonic beings, including even the arch-demon Ahreman (PY 30.10, Dk5 8.11). Hyperonym of dēw (GBd 27.24). May be male or female (Dk5 24.22). Various otherwise unnamed druzān are distinguished by indication of their respective domain of influence (e.g., druz ī ahlomōγīh, druz ī sāstārīh, tišn druz, etc.). Often depicted as inside humans' bodies and linked to bodily impurity.
